response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems developer, but the profile diverges sharply from this role's core requirements. The position demands native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX embedded development, Linux kernel source modification, and L2/L3 networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X). The resume lists no Japanese proficiency, only brief C/C++ experience (six months at National Instruments), and no embedded networking, switch, or security-protocol work. The language requirement alone is disqualifying.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering career across web, mobile, and systems domains
Low-latency, high-throughput backend experience with async I/O and Linux
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement (native level)
Minimal C/UNIX experience and no Linux kernel modification background
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X) experience

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded C and Linux kernel experience
